name: scan
summary: The scan command is used to enter external 3D mesh data into the cave 3D model. This is particularly useful for integrating high-resolution LiDAR scans or photogrammetry models directly with other surveyed data.
syntax: scan file <filename> units <units> axes <x-spec> <y-spec> <z-spec> [calibration data] endscan

options.raw: file <filename> = filename in STL format containing 3D mesh data.
options.name: file <filename>
options.description: filename in STL format containing 3D mesh data.

options.raw: units <units> = units of the STL file. Metres by default.
options.name: units <units>
options.description: units of the STL file. Metres by default.

options.raw: axes <x-spec> <y-spec> <z-spec> = order of axes in the STL file. Axes can be in any order, you can use x, -x, y, -y, z or -z. A minus prefix means the axis is reversed.
options.name: axes <x-spec> <y-spec> <z-spec>
options.description: order of axes in the STL file. Axes can be in any order, you can use x, -x, y, -y, z or -z. A minus prefix means the axis is reversed.

options.raw: [calibration data] = multiple lines of coordinates mapping known survey stations to the 3D mesh. Each line contains position of specifin survey station in STL file coordinates in <station-name> <x> <y> <z> order.
options.name: [calibration data]
options.description: multiple lines of coordinates mapping known survey stations to the 3D mesh. Each line contains position of specifin survey station in STL file coordinates in <station-name> <x> <y> <z> order.
